summary:
Agar stands out as one of the more mucostatic impression materials because it causes less tissue irritation than other commonly used alternatives such as zinc oxide eugenol and alginate; however, it may require additional care during preparation. Given its reduced irritant effect, agar can be considered highly suitable when minimally invasive procedures involving soft tissues are required.
The most mucostatic impression material identified from available data is agar, which provides significant benefit by reducing tissue irritation relative to traditional options like zinc oxide eugenol and alginate, though requiring special handling techniques.
